title: Fix the CrashLoopBackOff
scenario: Your on-call rotation just fired. The `webapp` pod is stuck in CrashLoopBackOff. The previous engineer hard-coded `/wrong-command` as the container entrypoint during a rushed hotfix. Traffic is down. Fix the pod spec so the container starts and stays running.
starterYaml:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: webapp
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: webapp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Always
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:1.25
    command: ["/wrong-command"]
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80

objectives.description: Pod named `webapp` exists in the `default` namespace

hints.text: Look at the `command` field in the container spec. nginx's default entrypoint is `/docker-entrypoint.sh` — you usually don't need to override it.
The simplest fix: remove the `command` field entirely. nginx will use its own default entrypoint and start serving on port 80.
If you need a custom command, use: `command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "nginx -g 'daemon off;'"]`

solution:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: webapp
  namespace: default
  labels:
    app: webapp
spec:
  restartPolicy: Always
  containers:
  - name: app
    image: nginx:1.25
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
